key_drivers: Rising real interest rates and VIX
reasoning: The macro picture is mixed. On one hand, the yield curve is currently upward sloping (normal), which signifies expectations of economic expansion [57205ed3e4b4], and the 10y real rate is low relative to history (24th percentile), which typically increases liquidity and reduces volatility in risk assets [99ad6cdde172]. However, there are conflicting short-term trends: the yield curve is falling (flattening), which is associated with expectations of economic contraction [e0d011fb7082]. Simultaneously, real interest rates are rising (3m +0.28 pp), which leads to decreased borrowing and liquidity [57731d675323], and this rise in real money costs correlates with the observed increase in equity market volatility (VIX rising 1m +4.3) [1f895bc8e557].
